Objective: Verify transactional templates are displayed in the saved templates list
Conditions:
1. User is logged in with messaging integration
2. User has transactional templates
3. Experiment mctx_streamlined_tx_template_management is enabled (variation_1)
4. Navigate to /templates?tab=saved
Steps to Test:
1. Navigate to Email templates page (/templates?tab=saved)
2. Observe template list
3. Identify templates marked as transactional
Expected Results:
1. Transactional templates are visible in the list
2. Transactional templates have visual indicator/badge
3. Templates show correct status (draft/published)

Objective: Verify format and status columns display correctly for transactional templates
Conditions:
1. User has transactional templates
2. Experiment is enabled
3. Navigate to saved templates tab
Steps to Test:
1. Navigate to /templates?tab=saved
2. Observe table columns
3. Check format and status for transactional templates
Expected Results:
1. Format column shows 'Transactional' for tx templates
2. Status column shows 'Draft' or 'Published'
3. Columns are sortable/filterable

Objective: Verify dedicated Transactional section/filter exists on templates page
Conditions:
1. User has messaging integration
2. Experiment is enabled
3. Navigate to templates page
Steps to Test:
1. Navigate to /templates
2. Look for Transactional filter/tab/section
3. Click on Transactional filter
Expected Results:
1. Transactional filter/section is visible
2. Clicking filter shows only transactional templates
3. Marketing templates are hidden when filter is active
Notes: Filter was not visible on the templates page. Appears to be missing from the UI.

Objective: Verify Transactional status filter works in Saved templates tab
Conditions:
1. User has mix of marketing and transactional templates
2. Experiment is enabled
Steps to Test:
1. Navigate to /templates?tab=saved
2. Locate Transactional status filter
3. Apply filter
4. Verify results
Expected Results:
1. Transactional status filter is present
2. Filter options include transactional statuses
3. Applying filter shows correct subset of templates
Notes: Filter dropdown shows incorrect options - missing 'Draft' and 'Published' states

Objective: Verify user can create transactional template from pre-built templates
Conditions:
1. User has messaging integration
2. Experiment is enabled
3. Navigate to Acme Platform templates tab (/templates?tab=mc)
Steps to Test:
1. Navigate to /templates?tab=mc
2. Select 'Transactional' tag from TagBar
3. Hover over a transactional template option
4. Click 'Create template'
Expected Results:
1. Pre-built transactional templates are displayed
2. Hovering shows 'Create template' action
3. Clicking opens template in Nuni editor
4. New template is marked as transactional

Objective: Verify templates created from pre-built transactional options are marked as transactional
Conditions: 1. User creates template from pre-built transactional option
Steps to Test:
1. Create template from pre-built transactional template
2. Save the template
3. Verify template metadata
4. Check templates list
Expected Results:
1. Newly created template has is_transactional='Y'
2. Template appears in transactional section/filter
3. Template has correct default settings

Objective: Verify 'Change template' button is hidden when editing transactional template
Conditions:
1. User has transactional template
2. Experiment is enabled (variation_1)
3. Template is opened in Nuni editor
Steps to Test:
1. Open transactional template in Nuni editor
2. Click on 'Save and exit' combo button dropdown
3. Look for 'Change template' option
Expected Results:
1. 'Save and exit' dropdown appears
2. 'Change template' option is NOT present
3. Other options (Save, Save and exit) remain available
Notes: 'Change template' option IS visible in dropdown - should be hidden for transactional templates

Objective: Verify 'Change template' button IS visible for marketing (non-transactional) templates
Conditions:
1. User has marketing template
2. Experiment is enabled
3. Template is NOT transactional
Steps to Test:
1. Open marketing template in Nuni editor
2. Click on 'Save and exit' combo button dropdown
3. Look for 'Change template' option
Expected Results:
1. 'Save and exit' dropdown appears
2. 'Change template' option IS present
3. Marketing template editing is not affected

Objective: Verify 'Changes not published' overlay appears for draft transactional templates
Conditions:
1. Transactional template has unsaved/unpublished changes
2. Template was previously published
Steps to Test:
1. Make changes to published transactional template
2. Preview the template
3. Observe overlay message
Expected Results:
1. Preview shows 'Changes not published' overlay
2. Overlay is clearly visible
3. User understands changes are in draft state

Objective: Verify transactional template preview works in Nuni editor
Conditions:
1. Transactional template is open in Nuni editor
2. Template has content including merge tags like *|FNAME|*
Steps to Test:
1. Click preview button (eye icon) in Nuni editor toolbar
2. Wait for preview panel to load
3. Click mobile/desktop toggle buttons
4. Observe how merge tags are rendered
Expected Results:
1. Preview panel opens within 2 seconds showing rendered template
2. Desktop view shows full-width email layout
3. Mobile view shows responsive narrow-width layout
4. Merge tags display as literal text (e.g., '*|FNAME|*') or with placeholder text like '[First Name]'
5. No broken images or layout issues in preview

Objective: Verify transactional template checklist guides user through publish requirements
Conditions:
1. User is creating/editing transactional template
2. Experiment is enabled
Steps to Test:
1. Navigate to transactional template checklist page
2. Review checklist items
3. Complete required fields
Expected Results:
1. Checklist displays all required fields
2. Fields include: from_email, from_name, subject
3. Incomplete fields are highlighted
4. Publish button is disabled until requirements met

Objective: Verify user can publish transactional template from checklist page
Conditions:
1. Transactional template has all required fields filled
2. Template content is valid
3. Checklist shows all items complete
Steps to Test:
1. Fill all required checklist fields
2. Click Publish button
3. Confirm publish action
Expected Results:
1. Publish button becomes enabled when ready
2. Clicking publish triggers publish action
3. Template status changes to Published
4. Success message is displayed
Notes: Publish button stays disabled even when all fields are filled. Console shows JavaScript error.

Objective: Verify individual default fields can be edited from transactional checklist
Conditions:
1. Transactional template checklist is displayed
2. Template has some fields populated
Steps to Test:
1. Navigate to transactional checklist page
2. Locate from_email field and click the edit (pencil) icon
3. Change value from 'old@example.com' to 'new@example.com'
4. Click Save button
5. Refresh the page and verify the field value
Expected Results:
1. Edit icon appears next to from_email, from_name, and subject fields
2. Clicking edit opens inline text input or modal for editing
3. Save button confirms the change with visual feedback (checkmark or success message)
4. After refresh, from_email shows updated value 'new@example.com'
5. Invalid email format (e.g., 'notanemail') shows red error message 'Please enter a valid email address'

Objective: Verify appropriate zero state when user has no transactional templates
Conditions:
1. User has messaging integration
2. User has no transactional templates
3. Experiment is enabled
Steps to Test:
1. Navigate to transactional templates section/filter
2. Observe zero state display
Expected Results:
1. Zero state message is displayed
2. Message explains transactional templates
3. CTA to create first transactional template is present

Objective: Verify templates page is fully keyboard navigable
Steps to Test:
1. Tab through all interactive elements
2. Verify focus indicators are visible
3. Access all template actions via keyboard
4. Navigate transactional filter via keyboard
Expected Results:
1. All interactive elements are reachable via Tab
2. Focus indicators are clearly visible
3. Enter/Space activates buttons and links
4. Escape closes modals/dropdowns
Notes: Focus indicators not visible on several interactive elements. Tab order skips the filter dropdown.

Objective: Verify transactional filter is accessible via screen reader (Note: Gregory Lehman will do this)
Conditions:
1. Screen reader is enabled (VoiceOver/NVDA)
2. Navigate to templates page
Steps to Test:
1. Navigate to transactional filter using screen reader
2. Listen to announcements
3. Activate filter
4. Verify results are announced
Expected Results:
1. Filter has accessible name/label
2. Filter state (selected/not selected) is announced
3. Template count is announced after filtering
4. Results are navigable via screen reader
Notes: Filter has no accessible label. Screen reader announces 'button' with no context. State changes are not announced.
